title: Reset the active player line to the confirmed 0.5.0 baseline
summary: 0.6.0 does not widen the player surface. It promotes the operator-confirmed 0.5.0 code into a fresh semantic line, and restarts package and artifact naming under FrameAngel.DevPlayer with dev_cua_player and dev_plugin_player filenames so the next baseline does not alias older cached builds.
reasoning: The last stable player truth for this lane is the confirmed 0.5.0 rung. A fresh semantic boundary is needed both to avoid VaM cache ambiguity and to give the next feature work a clean base instead of continuing from the later autoplay-repair line. Restarting the package and internal artifact names at the same boundary also makes AddonPackages cleanup and future baseline tracking much easier.

changes.summary: Carries forward the confirmed 0.5.0 runtime and packaging behavior unchanged under a fresh 0.6.0 semantic identity.
changes.reasoning: This preserves the last known-good player behavior while making future additions explicit and reviewable.

changes.summary: Marks the new active branch as a clean post-0.5.0 restart point for the next bounded feature slices.
changes.reasoning: The player line can now re-add wanted features intentionally instead of dragging the 0.5.x repair churn forward.

changes.summary: Restarts the package identity at FrameAngel.DevPlayer.1.var and uses dev_cua_player.<version>.assetbundle plus dev_plugin_player.<version>.dll as the internal shipped filenames.
changes.reasoning: A new package and artifact namespace keeps the confirmed baseline from colliding with older cached Player-line deployments.

verification: Confirm the 0.6.0 branch is byte-for-byte the 0.5.0 player code except for version stamps and the new changelog boundary.
Build the next baseline package as FrameAngel.DevPlayer.1.var and confirm the packaged asset and plugin paths use dev_cua_player.0.6.0.assetbundle and dev_plugin_player.0.6.0.dll.
Use 0.6.0 as the clean source branch for the next bounded feature slice instead of continuing from the later 0.5.x repair branches.
knownIssues: 0.6.0 is a semantic reset boundary, not a new feature build.
Wanted post-0.5.0 features still need to be reapplied intentionally from this clean line.

Later note from 0.6.6: this reset boundary carried forward the 0.5.0 seek-resume timing seam unchanged, so it should not be read as preserving 0.4.33's intended delayed post-seek resume behavior for problematic media.
